name: good_m4_00_autonomy_vs_beneficence_business_ethics
premise: A DEI report leaked early; executives disagree on whether to reaffirm targets or pause public commitments during litigation. In Business Ethics, the situation tagged internally around autonomy vs beneficence business ethics has converged with local pressure in the Marlow Junction corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Counsel insists any public statement match what is already provable from retained logs, not what leadership believes is true. Key facts are still arriving from the field; the team is working off stale dashboards and verbal handoffs. Regulators or auditors are en route with a ninety-minute ETA; the first documented response will anchor every later question. The organization’s past incidents make short term feel morally loud today, while long term is the value that quietly erodes if you always choose expediency.

agents.name: Dr. Kim Park
agents.identity: I am Dr. Kim Park, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Business Ethics. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat long term as disposable collateral every time short term spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Nurse Lead Ana Ruiz
agents.identity: I am Nurse Lead Ana Ruiz, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Business Ethics. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so short term cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes long term in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will freeze the riskiest workflow steps and staff a manual bridge until equity and error-rate checks clear—trading speed for defensibility on long term.
I will let the fastest operational path proceed under heightened monitoring and rollback triggers—trading some certainty on long term for immediate traction on short term.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next irreversible system or legal step is imminent. Dr. Kim Park must choose under short term versus long term. What does Dr. Kim Park do?
